Requirement Text: SHALL implement rollback protection using monotonic versioning or secure counters.
SHALL preserve non-type CID bits for trace logging and scheme/profile handling, but SHALL NOT use them to change cryptogram type classification unless the licensed specification/profile requires it.
SHALL use PPSE 2PAY.SYS.DDF01 for contactless Entry Point.
SHALL return structured contactless outcome data rather than only generic approve/decline status.
SHALL evaluate contactless transaction limit, CVM limit, and floor limit using signed scheme/acquirer configuration.
SHALL treat CDCVM as profile-defined. The kernel SHALL NOT assume that any single tag or bit universally proves CDCVM without scheme/profile validation.
SHALL implement relay-resistance APDUs, timing constraints, and result handling only where the licensed profile requires or certifies them.
SHALL NOT treat CDCVM as a universal method code unless the active contactless profile explicitly defines that mapping.
SHALL construct PDOL, CDOL1, CDOL2, DDOL, and TDOL data by resolving each requested tag from the terminal/card/kernel context and appending the exact requested length.
SHALL use zero padding or profile-defined defaults only where permitted by EMV and scheme rules.
SHALL implement the full transition table as a machine-readable artifact with RFC 4180 valid CSV or JSON.
SHALL distinguish fatal protocol errors from risk conditions. A missing mandatory tag may be fatal in one state and TVR-mediated in another only if the licensed standard permits that behavior.
SHALL preserve enough context to replay a transaction deterministically for certification debugging, excluding clear PIN and excluded sensitive data.
SHALL process card removal, APDU timeout, host timeout, and callback failure through explicitly documented transitions.
SHALL construct CDOL1 and CDOL2 data exactly from the active DOL definition.
SHALL encode requested cryptogram type using 0x00, 0x40, or 0x80 request bits, plus only licensed/profile-defined lower-bit flags.
SHALL NOT use 0x80 as a "first GENERATE AC flag."
SHALL parse returned CID by CID & 0xC0 and shall parse AC, ATC, IAD, and any response template data according to EMV/profile rules.
SHALL build CDOL1 from tag 8C where present, or from profile-defined defaults where permitted.
SHALL include the unpredictable number, TVR, transaction amount, date, type, terminal country, CVM results, and other requested data exactly as CDOL1 requires.
SHALL request AAC, TC, or ARQC according to TAA.
SHALL parse response format 1 or 2 as permitted by the active profile.
SHALL if CDA was requested, verify CDA before accepting the returned cryptogram outcome.
SHALL separate contact and contactless kernel selection in configuration.
SHALL NOT treat Book C-8 as a contact kernel unless a specific licensed specification and certification approval explicitly support that use.
SHOULD use Book C-8 for contactless where the product, scheme set, region, terminal class, and certification scope support C-8.
